text: The surgeon uses the robotic ProGrasp instrument to position a bulldog clamp on the renal artery, with the fourth robotic arm stretching the renal hilar vessels for better access during dissection and clamping.

The surgeon clamps the renal vein for improved visualization of complex tumors. Using monopolar curved scissors, an incision is made circumferentially around the tumor along the scored mark on the renal capsule, including a margin of normal renal parenchyma. The robotic grasping instrument spreads along the line of resection for retraction and visualization. The assistant uses a suction tip to clear blood and apply counter traction on the renal parenchyma, delineating the dissection plane. The robotic grasper provides counter traction on the tumor during resection, ensuring careful handling to avoid trauma. The excised tumor is set aside for later retrieval.

The surgeon excises the upper pole tumor using monopolar curved scissors, following the previously scored mark on the renal capsule. The resection is performed circumferentially through the renal cortex, with upward traction applied to the tumor. The tumor is carefully excised and placed aside for later retrieval.
In the next video we demonstrate the techniques of reconstructing the inner layer of the resection bed,
